metadata.school_name: The University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

analysis.name: Adi Nester
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Nester is very nice and enthusiastic about what she teaches. She really cares about students understanding the materials. The lectures were very informative and engaging and the class discussions were interesting. Homework assignments were straightforward and most of the time she gave the answers already in class before we had to submit.

Dr. Nester was a very kind and understanding professor. She assigned lots of extremely lengthy readings which were the only things covered in lecture. Her exams were what you make of them, she is very clear with what will be on the exam, you just have to make sure to prepare for them well because she is a tough grader in my opinion.

I enjoyed this class! You can tell Nester is very passionate about the subjects, which sometimes led to going over time with lectures and getting off schedule. She really wants to help her students, she gave plenty of feedback on assignments and offered extra credit. She can be a little bit of a tough grader, but is clear with her expectations.

The class was very interesting and the lectures were well-organized with really helpful slides that made studying for the exams much more manageable. Prof. Nester is very nice and accommodating. She encouraged participation in class and gave a lot of helpful feedback on the homework assignments.

Professor Nester's class was wonderful. We read engaging texts from diverse perspectives, creating a rich and meaningful learning experience. Discussions were consistently thoughtful and in-depth. Exams were fair, and she clearly reviewed key material and how to prepare. Highly recommended.

analysis.name: Alain Aguilar
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Really enjoyable class, he teaches things multiple ways to accommodate different styles of learning, and is always happy to answer questions and hear what students are struggling with. Class is very centered around the needs of the students, and his learning checks and exams are written very fairly and do not try to trick you. Take his class!!

Favorite prof at Carolina. Class is so engaging and fun. He has many stories from his AT days. If you study you will get an A. That said I'm not done with the class but I have a 99 rn. Very accessible and so kind. You have learning checks and exit tickets that you get the dates for, so don't miss those classes. Such a fun class so show up.

EXSS 265! Have nothing but great things to say about this professor. He is so accommodating and noticed the needs of the students grades and offered extra credit. He has personal experience from being an AT, which was interesting to hear different stories. I would take this class and highly recommend and class with him!

(FOR 265) Prof Aguilar is very funny and understanding. The class is QUIZ HEAVY and it makes up most of your grade. They can easily be tricky so keep up with ULA reviews, go to class, take notes and ask questions. Hes very accessible to help you out and offered extra credit that rlly helps ur grade.

Took 265 with him. He was a very entertaining lecturer which made things easy to understand and remember. Class is very chill and easy. He's the type of professor who is happy if everyone gets an A. He has lots of stories from personal experience which are fun. He also keeps standing on top of his desk for no reason which is funny

analysis.name: Jocelyn Aksin
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Aksin is fantastic! Very nice and understanding, easy grader, and my favorite professor at UNC so far. I never felt like I had to be perfect to answer questions, and she never made learning feel like a chore. Some homework outside of class, but never anything crazy. Overall, very happy with Dr. Aksin, and am excited to take 204 with her!

My German made leaps and bounds under Dr.Askin. She learned German in middle school and as a result better understands the many struggles people learning German experience. She's able to break down complex German topics and make it easy to understand. Also very understanding, clearly hates to grade and doesn't put a lot of emphasis on it.

This is for GSLL 89 FYS. Dr. Aksin is amazing!!! She picks the most interesting literature/movies and gives fantastic lectures on super interesting topics. We think about language in a totally new way, and she has unique perspectives because of her background in Germany and Turkey. Highly recommend her seminar, and getting to know her as a person!

THIS IS FOR 203. Great professor, her lectures make learning a language easier, totally understanding and flexible, The entire class taught in German but makes sure to keep students in the loop and focused. Grading structure is favorable and encourages you to just come to class and learn. Would recommend to anyone trying to learn German

This seminar was really fun - small class size and pretty much all grades were very creative assignments (presentations and essays). Dr. Aksin speaks with no accent, and she does a lot to keep class topics interesting. This was the best type of environment to learn German in, and my favorite out of all of the German courses I've taken here so far.

analysis.name: Alecia Septer
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Genuinely the best professor I've taken at UNC. Her lectures are entertaining, and she's very passionate and knowledgeable about the content. She's also extremely accessible outside of class, through both email and her office hours. She had one midterm and the final; it was an extremely manageable course load. Can't recommend her classes enough

One of my favorite bio professors! Dr. Septer really cared about the success of her students, providing us with the BEST study guides and making herself available outside of class! I would definitely take a class with her again!

Dr. Septer is AMAZING! There are 16 quizzes, a midterm, and a final (but she drops the lowest 6 quizzes from your final grade!!!!) The class covers a lot of material, but the concepts aren't difficult. If you have any interest in marine science, I highly recommend meeting with her. She wants to help all of her students!
Really good professor who cares deeply about her students!

Fabulous lectures! Dr. Septer was passionate about the material she taught and made sure all students succeeded. There were weekly quizzes, all of which were open note with unlimited time to complete. She was also generous with quiz drops and exams were very reflective of classroom material. I recommend taking Dr. Septer if you need an easy class!

analysis.name: Andrey Smirnov
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 49 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Smirnov is one of the worst professors that I have had. Lecture was absolutely pointless b/c it made no sense. You would be better off being taught this class by a 5th grader. The midterms were impossible and there was no way to prepare b/c they had nothing to do with the hw, lecture, or anything in the book. The only help i ever got was from my TA

There's no point in going to his lecture because you come out more confused than you were when you went in. However, the textbook is awful so it's difficult to learn from if you try to read it. The homework questions are often different from those on the exam. He also talks very quietly and refuses to curve even when the average grade is failing.

Professor Smirnov is honestly a great guy. He is really caring and he knows his stuff. That being said he is awful. Lecture is impossible to follow, exams are unfair and long, he also goes really fast when covering material (we were always ahead of the other sections of 233). He does curve at the end which is cool but I would not recommend

His explanations aren't anything special, but I guess I've had worse. His grading is rather unfair and he makes exams too long for the time constraints for the purpose of "distinguishing" students who know material just as well as others but are faster, letting him maintain a clean Gaussian curve for grades. Not impressed.

Professor Smirnov is a very nice guy, however, Math 233 with him is near impossible. The averages on the tests are always failing grades and the lectures are almost pointless because they are so hard to follow. If you're good at learning on your own then you may be fine, but if not try another professor.

analysis.name: Alaina Garland
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 422 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Unless you want to learn random facts, such as the length of fruit fly's stuff or the debates around religion and evolution, do not take this course. Dr. Garland chooses to spend time on minor details that do not appear on the test, and thus does not effectively teach the class. Do not take BIO 101 with Dr. Garland. Save yourself the trouble.

I've never made one of these so you the fact that I had to take time to make this should be a warning. She has NO business teaching at the undergraduate level. If she taught Intro to ABCs I would not take it with her. I've had elementary teachers that are more capable of teaching than her. She is by far the WORST professor I have ever come across.

For starters, I hate this class. I took AP and honors Bio in high school and got As and then get a 36 on the first test. I would study for hours and the questions on the test did not make any sense what so ever. STAY CLEAR if you do not want to tank your GPA. She wastes time making a seating chart for a 400 person class instead of teaching.

Dr. Garland is such a sweetheart but you should steer clear of this course. The exams are harder than the material is to understand. And the lectures are fluffy and unhelpful. I'm hoping to be premed and this course made me genuinely reconsider whether I was fit for it. Do yourself a favor and take any other Professor. It's not worth the trouble.

Dr. Garland wastes a lot of lecture time going over useless information including but not limited to preaching about career goals. Tests are very tricky and seem to test test taking ability more rather than knowledge. Lecture lowkey useless, but she's definitely caring. Just not a great prof. Get ready to study and read the textbook.

analysis.name: Arunima Bhattacharya
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 22 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I actually love her. She's genuinely so smart, and this is a hard class, so I'm aware of the challenging content. But still, her lectures were great, and she stopped for questions a lot. The first midterm was definitely not easy, but she was generous with grading and made the second one easier. She's a very responsible teacher.

Dr. Bhattacharya was an amazing lecturer despite the abstractness of a lot of the concepts in real analysis. The subject was hard, but she was very fair and generous with grading, along with guidance outside of class during office hours or by email. Incredibly hard class, but she made it more tolerable.

The first exam average was 51%, which isn't unusual for MATH 521, but grading has been pretty unclear. We were only told a "nonuniform curve" will reduce the number of students who fail, leaving many students completely unsure how they will be affected by a curve. Lectures are generally good but often feel a bit rushed.
Amazing lecturer, she explained difficult topics super well. 521 was definitely a learning curve as it was different from other math classes I'd taken before but she made it easier to adapt.

This was a very tough class. Prof Bhattacharya is nice and understanding, but often did not explain class concepts fully, and homeworks were unreasonably hard. I learned more from my recitation TA. The final was near impossible, with the class average being an F. the class was curved a full letter grade at the end. I don't recommend this class.

analysis.name: Abel Munoz-Hermoso
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 92 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: To start, I had him for 355 (no option to select that on RMP) Abel is my favorite UNC profesor, this is the second class I've taken with him and 355 has been my favorite class by far. Reasonable workload, he is flexible, he is hilarious, class feels like a conversation, he made the entire class friends and I would talk to any of them outside10/10

Chill and understanding professor. Cares more about participation than perfection, make an effort to speak up in class and it'll go far, even if your Spanish isn't great. Communicate issues or a desire for an extension and he'll make it work for you, if he can tell that you care about the class.

I loved this man, very happy and go lucky, makes funny jokes. He makes class engaging and gives you a lot of freedom when it comes to your written work. Not a tough grader but expects participation in class. You can tell he has a passion for Spanish, teaching does not feel like a chore to him, cares about your learning. Reopened homeworks for me.

This guy is the goat. I took him and can honestly say he was one of the best Spanish teachers I have ever had. If you are a student you need to take him (like trust me). His lectures were fun, although the content is hard he makes it fun to learn, and really wants to see you succeed as a Spanish speaker and in the class.

Amazing class and professor. He cares a lot about his students and their success in the course, and he is very passionate about teaching. Grading, assignments, tests, and quizzes were very fair. My Spanish improved so much from this class, so if you are thinking about taking it, definitely take it with him!

analysis.name: Anne Rea
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 129 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Maybe it's just me, but I felt as though the exams weren't testing what we knew on the subject; rather, it felt like she was just trying to trick us with lengthy word questions. Extremely condescending in personal emails. Also no cheat sheet for the test when other teachers do allow it. Take Biol 103, just not with her...

Hasn't graded most of our written assignments and our third midterm yet and it's almost LDOC. There are very few resources to study for this course. The lecture slides barely have any written notes, and the exams are heavily based on them. I wish there wasn't a character limit because I still have more to say about how horrible this class was.

Wish I could cuss. Terrible and overly nuanced; you are expected to devote to her class like a cult if you even dream of doing well. The exams are a toss-up; the mean is always low, and the questions are unrelated to the lessons we are told to pay attention to. The 5 star review is 100% from the few people Rea is actually nice to. Condescending af

She seems sweet and considerate but she really isn't. Her timing is always off, we were consistently about 10 days behind, and she often posted assignments late with very little time to finish them. She assigned four tasks over Thanksgiving break and regularly makes assignments due on wellness days or days off. Overall, not the best Bio professor.

Focuses too much on irrelevant details during the lecture. Behind schedule since FDOC. Lessons skipped or moved asynchronously to "be caught up" (we never caught up). ALWAYS last minute. Unit quizzes due less than 24 hours before the exam. Has assignments due AFTER LDOC. Modules and assignments are not posted in a timely manner.

analysis.name: Lois Boynton
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 51 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Great, great professor. She's funny, discussions are interesting, and the readings are minimal (and interesting). I don't know why people say this class is so hard, it's really not as long as you put in the same amount of effort you would any other class. (It's easy not to because it seems like such easy material, but she's a tough grader).

She is a super sweet professor. She is easy to have a conversation with. My only complaint is it is nearly impossible to make anything higher than 90. I have gotten maybe 2 grades over 90, and heard this is common for other students. We don't do much in class, but from what we do it is an interesting and engaging class.

Dr. Boynton is so sweet and funny! The content can be a little boring at times, but she always managed to make us smile during class. Daily in-class group assignments were pretty easy and useful. Sometimes, she can be a tough grader, but she genuinely cares about her students and loves her job.

Lois is so funny and gives entertaining lectures. Cares about her students and is accessible outside of class! Can be a tough grader at times but she cares about her students succeeding, especially in a 100 level class.

I want to love Dr. B, but I am not happy with the experience I had this past semester. She uses her lecture time as a standup comedy hour. She is hilarious, but I learned approximately 25 percent of what I wanted to learn. My classmates and I agreed that we were teaching ourselves by the end of the semester. Take 141 with someone else.

analysis.name: Karen Booth
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 83 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Do not take a class with Dr. Booth. She is unorganized, has very inconsistent and random deadlines ex. 4:15, and unclear grading policies. She randomly canceled class and added assignments at the last minute. This would not be such an issue but she was inconsiderate of student's needs, even if there were issues with tech or health (in a pandemic).

Do not take this class. Dr. Booth uses lectures not to challenge students, but to find students who validate her own world view. The course does have very interesting readings, but you can do them in your free time. She is also incredibly trans-exclusive and has disorganized lectures. She is not willing to explain confusing course assignments.

Not impressed at all. Class "discussions" entailed her calling on different people until she got the answer she wanted. She is very self-righteous & actively shut me down when I tried to bring up trans health issues in a sexual health class. Reading materials were interesting but I would suggest reading them on your own time.

All you do is sit in a circle and discuss the readings, and all she does is shut down conversation and make people feel like they said something "wrong." by halfway through the semester we all just sat in a silent, tense circle because everyone was too afraid to talk. Really interesting readings though, I did learn a lot from them.

Do not take a class with Karen. She has impossibly high standards for participation, and her grading is incredibly subjective. She WILL give you a bad grade if she doesn't like you. These "subjective" grades make up 80% of your total grade. You can participate everyday and still get an 80% for participation for no reason. Such a subjective grader!

analysis.name: JJ Bauer
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Please do not take this class if you are looking for an easy gen ed. I worked so incredibly hard for the B+ that I go. Professor Bauer is kind, but her lectures are incredibly disorganized and the amount of material included on midterms is insane. Get ready to study and memorize your heart out if you take this class.

Very difficult considering it is an introductory level class. Her lectures go incredibly fast and are very disorganized to the point where I had to reorganize all my notes after every lecture. Midterms and exams are written only and require very dense knowledge about the structures covered and memorization of terms, dates, and architects.

This class was a lot of fun! I think this class is one that you can take regardless of your level of interest in film, because the movies were all very good and the lectures were extremely interesting! Just get ready to write a few papers, but overall the work is very manageable and the professor is so nice and accommodating!

JJ is one of the best professors I've had at UNC so far. This was a rough semester, but JJ's class was a bright spot. I'm not an art major and have never been the creative type, but the material in the lectures was pretty interesting and she is good at grasping students' attention. Great class and great prof
This is a great professor and a great course to take some time here at UNC. Made learning more about the art styles in movies very entertaining and had good commentary on the aspects of movies.

analysis.name: George Baroud
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Baroud is easily one of my favorite professors. His class was immensely difficult (300 pages of dry, dense reading each week; could not bullshit your way through exams), but his grading was fair and he forces you to grow as a thinker/reader. He's scary (and maybe a bit snarky) at first, but he is super approachable in office hours.

Don't take this class if you want to sit by, skip the readings, and hope you'll get a good grade. Professor Baroud is an amazing professor, extremely intelligent, and loves the Roman Empire. Highly recommend going to office hours, as he wants to know every student personally. If you are willing to go a little crazy, then this is the class for you.

Quite literally the most engaging and thought provoking lectures I've ever heard. He's an incredibly knowledgable and charismatic professor. Main downside of the class are the reading assignments, almost too lengthy to handle. If you show up to all the lectures, do the readings, and ask questions, you'll do well. I wish I could take CLAS122 again.
Professor Baroud is an amazing storyteller and professor. I got a B and still would take the class again because of how well he taught the course.

Prof is very passionate, intelligent and gives good lectures. I wouldn't recommend this class as a gen ed unless you actually like the Romans and classics. Readings are long and confusing ~150-200 pages a week but he explains the readings in class. Tests were graded kinda harsh but office hours helped me improve.

analysis.name: Pauline Blaimont
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Blaimont was amazing! This class had a lot of content and work, but her lectures and teaching style always prepared me. Her exams were difficult in the right ways, testing things in an understandable manner from lectures and work. 220 had a lot of content, but I feel like she made it as manageable as possible. Take her if you have the chance!

Dr. Blaimont was probably the best 220 professor ever. I was so terrified for this class and she was the nicest person ever. Her lectures were clear and engaging, and didn't mind explaining something slowly in class when I asked for help. Office hours are great, exams are fair, and she is hilarious! Don't miss the chance to take a class with her!

Blaimont is an amazing professor. She is very kind, very enthusiastic about the subject (she is young and relatively new), and very straightforward about what she wants from her students. She is also very accessible outside of class for questions, help, and anything else. 100% the best option to take for 220!

God-tier of a professor. Definitely take Dr. Blaimont for BIOL 220! Very fair exams and grading on assignments. Patient on answering questions. I wish she would answer questions more directly, but she is already doing an amazing job for her 1st semester at UNC! Study the ppt and journal club, then you will be fine!

Blaimont is awesome. Her lectures are interesting and she is great at explaining things. Expectations for assignments are clear and exams are fair. She is very knowledgable and you can tell she cares. I definitely recommend taking her!

analysis.name: Barbara Osborne
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Overall, a good professor but the class material is boring, it's based around a lot of readings and court cases, so she can only make it so interesting. The quizzes online for each chapter can be difficult but are not too hard if you go to class and pay attention. Quizzes could probably be done w/o going to class but would definitely be harder.

I took her EXSS326 summer class and she rocks. She's really funny and gets to know all her students really well. She tells personal stories related to the law that are actually interesting, and she will help you out if you're ever confused or having trouble with anything. Participation is a big part of her class.
Not an easy course, but with 100% online quizzes you can do well on them. Attendance is optional and overall just boring lectures.

You will not regret taking Professor Osborne! She is hands down one of the best professors I have ever had the privilege of taking. I highly recommend her course and she will always do whatever it takes to make you successful in the course. She definitely cares about her students. The course has challenging aspects of it as it is a law class.

I loved Prof. Osborne! Truly caring for her students. Her 326 class is graded 85% on open-book, open note quizzes and 15% class participation, so doing well on the quizzes really matters. I was having trouble with the quizzes for the first part of the semester and started emailing her specific quiz questions and it saved my grade. So helpful!

analysis.name: Brian Brew
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Hes a Goat. Always made it so fun to show up and as a student who is the type to sit in the back and be on there phone I was front row always paying attention to him. He does expect effort, but with how engaging his teaching is its impossible not to put in effort. Chill grader, genuinely funny guy, and great political takes. Made me want an A

Incredible professor with true passion and inspiration for his students. Gave so much grace when I struggled due to personal reasons and let me take the midterm a week late. Such an incredible professor, take his classes!!!! So funny and attendance isn't graded (but it's important unless u look at the notes) No electronics but so interesting.

brian was a pretty easy grader. one 10-12 page paper, a midterm, a final, and a couple of assignments that required you to go to office hours for a bit. he was pretty nice but he did go on random rants about primaries where he screamed for like 5 mins. there are lots of readings but you can just skim them enough to write the discussion posts
He is a great professor that is easily reached outside of class and cares about the success of his students. I highly recommend you take a class of his if possible.

Brian is pretty great. He clearly loves political science, and that is shown in his lectures. He isn't a very harsh grader, and if you are interested in political science, you will enjoy his lectures. POLI 208 had two papers, a midterm and a final exam, and none of them were too difficult. It's a good class to learn in and not worry about grades!

analysis.name: John Bruno
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: My favorite professor so far at UNC. His exam was so easy and was straight from the study guide. His lectures were interesting and he sometimes brought his dog to class. This course is taught with two other professors and he by far was the easiest one.

This is one of the best professor's I have ever had, he really cares about his students and is very passionate and knowledgeable about this course and topics. Definitely take this class, very easy and super interesting.

Dr. Bruno was by far my favorite lecturer on the FoF teaching team. His lectures were insightful and easy to follow, and he always brings his dog Mr. Lizard to class when he teaches. He gives an in-depth synopsis of the fishing industry and the fraud within it. He is also accessible outside of class and offers advice who show the initiative to ask.

Dr. Bruno was the best professor in FoF. He made lectures very interesting and was always knowledgable on what he was doing. Hes funny and is always very kind to the students. FoF as a class wasn't too hard but the quizzes were very detailed so I reccommend at least reviewing before. Everything else was graded easily, including the final paper.

One of the best professor ever met! He really supports and wants student to learn how to do research and understand sustainability related topics. Grade is not a concern for this class (he's grading fairly and  leniently) but understanding science & research is the goal.

analysis.name: Brandon Bayne
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 53 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: The class itself wasn't that much work: 10 discussion posts and replies, 2 quizzes, 1 midterm paper and a final project. But Bayne had no rubrics, barely any guidelines for assignments, and grading was random and subjective. He also didn't grade the majority of our assignments until the last week of class which was stressful and frustrating.

This was my favorite course so far at Carolina. It made me reflect on my own faith and consider ways we could be doing better. Professor Bayne is super knowledgeable about both Christianity and Native history. Sometimes it was tough to confront so many difficult moments, but he always gave us helpful context and led creative group discussions.

this class was a gen ed for me but i loved this class. prof and TA are awesome and i learned a lot. not a lot of hw for the class, probably an 1-2 each week but readings are required and they do notebook checks. exams were easy and theres xtra assignments to pad out ur grade. overall a super nice guy who wants to see students succeed!

I LOVED this class and the teacher. He is a great lecturer and I was never bored in his class. He is very clear on his assignments and exams. If you put in the work, this is a very easy class to get an A in. he gives and adequate amount of work. There is a lot of readings but they are enjoyable. He also made us food for the exam.

Dr. Bayne and Carla were amazing this semester. The classroom environment is very engaging and interesting, and both are very caring. As long as you pay attention and study the exam guides, an A is nearly guaranteed. The best professor and TA duo I have had at UNC so far.

analysis.name: Corey Johnson
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 129 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Awesome professor. As he says "Just know everything and you will be fine". Biol 252 is a had class, but if I would have to take it all over again, I would take it with him. I would take any possible class with him, loved him. He is a great teacher!

I spent more time on this than on the actual BIOL 252 course. This is unforgiving, relentless, and extremely poorly designed for it to be a 1 credit lab. Honestly, it wouldn't be much of a problem if it didn't have the RANDOM application questions that makeup 50 questions on each exam. No teaching is done, this is purely a test of your memorization

Each exam is worth 20%, there are 2 non-cumulative exams. They consist of 50 identification open-response questions (no word bank) and 50 application questions (random and open-response). By each exam date, you should have memorized roughly 150 terms, this class only consists of memorization. The final is cumulative and 30% of your grade.

Challenging class.  Soooooo much material, but you'll thank him later. Really great lectures.  There's some of the talk-to-your-neighbor BS like other bio classes but not too much.  This course helped me learn to READ, and I'm thankful for it.

Professor Johnson is the best professor I have ever had. Take any and all courses you can with him. Heck, if he was teaching Intro to Women's Studies or History of Medieval Europe I would take that class. The subject matter is difficult for A&P, but he makes it interesting. The assignments are easy. The multiple-choice exams are not. Study a lot.

analysis.name: Joseph Compton
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 89 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Curved 2 midterms, then told us we were “cooked” right before starting the final, then dropped the curve entirely. No grading scale, no clear policy, and final scores hidden until grades were locked. Some secret points added to the final after complaints. Grading felt arbitrary, dismissive, and shady. If you value fairness, avoid this professor.

Took him for Calc 2 and really liked him, so decided to take for discrete and regretted it. I know discrete is supposed to be hard, but not this hard. Homework is impossible to do with going to office hours and/or using AI/internet (and even then still incredibly hard). Test questions are also unnecessarily difficult. Don't recommend taking him.

Difficult and time consuming homework, no practice exams or study guide, not clear on what he puts on midterms. Grading was harsh and unfair. He's fine in lecture, but makes the class entirely more difficult than it needs to be. Random quizzes to count for attendance & only drops one to account for sickness, so if you have to miss more you're SOL.

The assignments are vague. The lectures are scattered and unstructured. We didn't have a syllabus for the first month of class.

Although this class is online, she is very inaccessible. Participation is graded on timely submissions, discussion posts, and readings. I've sent her multiple emails and never responded to them. She leaves undetailed feedback on assignments and takes forever to grade as well. Class is online, but it isn't easy when she's basically nonexistent!!!!!

I enjoyed the class a lot. She is a very nice professor who is intelligent and caring. I like her teaching style, and she often shares her anecdotes in class(which are amazing!). Probably the smartest lecturer I have seen. She has a Ph.D. in information science, MPA, and Juris Doctor degree. Accessible outside classes.

analysis.name: Emily Burkhead
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 249 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: The class itself is not horrible, you can preview by looking up "understanding analysis" and practice. Tests decide your grade, not hard if you know how to apply a theorem or prove it. She hates to grade higher for those who improved, and she grades harsh, bonus and curve are almost nothing, I regret taking her hunger game class only for a minor.

Taking Calculus 3 with this professor was the worst experience of my life. Studying for her exams brought me to tears. The test study guides are an extremely poor representation of the exams and she wrote most of the final so her students did bad on that too. Her class average is a C, while others are an A. Do not take this class! Save your GPA!!!

Carefully assess your abilities. There's no curve for this class at all. It's the only one among 233 courses that requires a weekly exam. Also, I've seen exams from other sections that had only five simple questions in 50 minutes, but Professor Burkhead's test contained more than 20 challenging and time-consuming problems, with no extra time given.

Save your grade and don't take 381 with Burkhead. Inaccessible and hard to approach with questions related to grading or asking her to explain concepts. Asked her many times to explain the material, and was often met with snarky remarks. Office hours are even worse, as she is not much help if you're lost.

I gave her a 4/5 when I took her for MATH233. That was a lie. Tests and homework were graded harshly which would have been fine if she curved generously. Given what she said is "on track for an A/B/C" after each midterm, the curve should have been like +13% when it was actually +3%. Ragebait incarnate.

analysis.name: Eren Tasar
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 93 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor Tasar is an incredible professor. He is absolutely brilliant with two degrees from Harvard and one from Stanford. But more than that, he is one of the most genuine, thoughtful, and hilarious professors I have ever encountered. He makes topics come to life with his charm and enthusiasm. 10/10 take a class with him--you will not regret it.

He's great and lectures aren't boring. Only took class for geneds and I learned a lot from him. There is a lot of reading but he understands the college struggle. Also, for the final he was open for people to ask him questions and he responded almost immediately through emails despite it being really late at night

Professor Tasar was by far my favorite professor at UNC so far. He is very personable and even stopped the class to escort a student to CAPS because he wanted them to get help when they showed up to class crying. All of the readings are very interesting and he never assumes that he is correct. He is okay with being corrected and proven wrong.

Fall 2025. Class of 240 ppl, and lectures were recorded and posted on Canvas, so attendance very much optional. Lectures fairly engaging, though the reading was a bit odd: a series of novels related to the content. Quizzes were sometimes on material not covered until the following week. Exams weren't bad, but grading heavily dependent on your TA.

Professor Tasar is one of my favorite history professors! He clearly cares about the material he teaches and the success of his students. Lots of reading, but the books are generally engaging. While this class is called the history of 9/11, the first half of the course was dedicated to understanding Islam while the second half focused on terrorism.

analysis.name: Sara Estle
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 51 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Estle was an amazing professor. She truly wants you to succeed in her class, and if you need help understanding a concept, she takes the time to help. She is so kind and is always high-energy, but not in a corny way. She is sunlight personified, and truly understands her craft. If I could, I would take every single class with her!

Easiest NSCI prof & class ever. Her lectures drag quite a bit and she is not super engaging, but she is extremely kind and cares about student success. The exams can be a bit tricky, but her learning checks are gold for exam prep. The final is optional if you are happy with your grade and the homeworks/quizzes are super easy. Very easy A.

Dr. Estle is incredible. You can tell she really wants you to learn, and cares about the subject she teaches. The content is difficult, but she makes it very approachable. Final is optional (replaces lowest exam grade) and there are plenty of assignments other than exams to help your grade. Please take this class with Dr. Estle, she's the best!

Dr. Estle is one of the best and kindest professors I have had at UNC. She makes otherwise tough content really approachable and her teaching style and sense of humor make class a really welcoming environment. However, you do have to attend lecture because she adds a lot of things that are key for exams but aren't on the slides.

Genuinely the sweetest prof, she truly cares about her students and the content she teaches. For 220, she always tries to incorporate an interactive learning activity that helps most students learn the content better. She is very considerate, and her class quickly became my fav over this semester.

analysis.name: Evyatar Marienberg
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 39 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: (Fall 2025) Loved this class. Marienberg can be a bit of a character (in a good way!). Absolutely hilarious guy, great stories, and still gets you immersed in the content. Grading was very easy: mostly completion-based, and what wasn't was fine if you skimmed the reading and paid attention. Loved the course and professor. Will take him again.

Not sure why the ratings are so low, I had a great experience with Professor Marienberg. He's very understanding and his lectures were very engaging. There is often a good amount of reading per week and a discussion post or quiz for every class. The midterm and final were relatively easy as long as you studied the concepts and reading material.

Nice man, though he was difficult to reach. Class content consists of readings and discussion posts due every class which, if done well, will take multiple hours each. The lectures are basically a recap of these. Exams contain very specific knowledge, but if you actually do the readings (and not just put them in ChatGBT), they're very manageable.

Dr. Evyatar has a wealth of knowledge and shares it enthusiastically. He's honestly a funny guy and shows videos in class, along with bringing in prayer shawls. Classwork is easy, you just write a discussion post before each class based on the readings.

Prof Marienberg is super funny and a great guy! His classes are lectures, but he is very funny and adds videos and time to discuss the topics with your classmates, which helps break up the lectures. He is very accessible and helpful outside of class. Great class!

analysis.name: Erica Johnson
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 39 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Assigns 80-100 pages of dense, philisophical, and academic writing each week. In fact, the readings are so dense that she spends the entirety of class having to break them down for us. Little attempt to make the material relateable or exciting. In recitations, we sometimes sit in silence for over 30 minutes. Shocked at how high her ratings are.

I took Dr. Johnson's class last year FA'24. This class was very interesting and honestly the heavy reading was worth it because she would go over them in class. You should take notes though so you can review for the midterms and final. The midterm exam was a little harshly graded (all of the grades depend on TA) , but otherwise, good class.

Paid so little attention I had to look up this prof' name to review. And still got an A. The lectures are a direct repeat of reading: there is no new information. Don't skip the reading, it's more important and detailed than lecture. A few times we watch movie in lecture, ATTEND. Only hard part is midterm.  Easy A, just actually go to recitation.

Honestly, this class was not enjoyable. The writing style for the essays is unintuitive. Instead of making our own arguments, we're forced to base everything we write on the sources given in class. There is a LOT of reading, but you can just skim them, she goes over them in depth during lectures. The lectures about economics were sooo boring.

Dr Johnson is a caring prof but this class was not my fav even as a global studies major. It's organized well but the way it's taught was very vague/broad, which could be an issue of the course itself. She's a fair grader and most of the test is from the readings she assigns - which is a lot so keep up w/ them. There are disc q's and 2 essays.

analysis.name: Elliott Kuecker
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Elliot is such an amazing professor. He is passionate about his students and what he teaches. He is very personable, has engaging lectures, and assigns projects that are impactful!
Attendance is a must! I missed a few classes over the limit was penalized. But he is a great prof who cares about his students success.

Dr. Kuecker is literally the best professor I've ever had. He builds incredibly well-organized online classes, so there is no excuse to miss an assignment--you can work ahead which is so nice. He also is just one of the nicest people you will meet and clearly cares about his students. If you show up and do the work, you will learn so much from him!

An awesome professor. He really cares about 1) what he's teaching and 2) his students. The assignments are fun and engaging, as are the in-class discussions. Assigned readings average at about 3 per week. He also likes to share relevant events and employment/internship/award opportunities, which is a plus. Again, great professor!

Professor Kuecker has a great reputation at UNC SILS as being of the few professors who not only teaches real-world skills but is approachable, friendly, and helpful. He goes above and beyond for the students, and everyone would benefit by taking as many of his classes as possible. 20/10 DO recommend.

analysis.name: Toni Formato
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: kind, genuine, and supportive person who understands the struggle of students. super open about her own experiences and listens if you need help with anything/deadlines. she will sit and wait for someone to speak if no one is responding to her questions but she gives good feedback for any answer. would recommend again and again!

She was a wonderful teacher and support system at UNC. She makes it very clear that she cares about every one of her students, inside and outside of the classroom. If you have the opportunity to take a class with her, do it!
She's awesome!! I love the fact that she can relate to us, since she went to UNC.

analysis.name: Sarah Ficke
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 27 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: She always grades things based off of a scale of "does not meet expectations, meets expectations, and exceeds expectations". Iit is not clear what each of these even means and she literally never gives exceeds expectations right off the bat meaning she makes you redo literally every single assignment. I do not recommend taking this class.

Sarah Ficke is a nice person and typically grades pretty fairly. However, her lectures are extremely boring and difficult to pay attention in. She also doesn't grade assignments on a normal 1-100 scale, but rather "exceeds" or "meets" or "does not meet expectations" which makes it difficult to gauge how you're doing in the class.

Dr. Ficke is very kind and accessible outside of class. The way the class is graded is on a grading contract, which can be frustrating as you can't really be sure of your grade until the midterm check-up and final grade are released. Class can be sorta boring at times, but she is very nice, and there is a lot of time to get feedback in class.

She's a nice person, like a grandma but she's one of the worst teachers I've ever had. Absolutely no form of grading criteria and she has a "grading contract" instead of normal grades. She makes a bunch of convoluted rules surrounding each assignment. Never knew my grade, and she hated giving feedback. Class was really slight, just annoying.

Professor Ficke is really chill. Her lectures can get a bit boring sometimes but the work is pretty easy. The feeders can be quite tedious sometimes but she does allow you to resubmit your assignments if you don't get the grade you want. You can only see your grade once in the middle of the semester and once at the end.

analysis.name: Gregory Forest
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: This review is actually for Math 528. Greg was wonderful, he genuinely cares about his students and wants everyone to do well. Exams are just like the practice but with different numbers. Our second midterm and 4/5 of the final was take-home. Do the work and you'll be fine. He's super approachable and accommodating! Overall great person and prof.

Greg is a hilarious professor and his jokes made lectures entertaining. His teaching style wasn't for me (he tried to cover as many examples as possible rather than explaining what he was doing). This class is extremely test heavy, but he gives decent review sheets. At the end he changed the syllabus so everyone got the highest grade possible!

Very sweet man.. very hard class. I could definitely tell he was not used to teaching 231 because a lot of things did not make sense and he did a poor job explaining. Course moves very fast and it is quite easy to fall behind due to old school teaching style. Don't purchase textbook, it's free online. He does not have office hours-relies on TAs.

One of my best classes ever. Although I had taken calc in hs this was a  much harder and required studying. He made sure to explain each concept and for our final exam he went through each students grades to adjust scores to make sure we got the highest grade we could also also rounded many people. Would 100% recommend taking calc 1 with him.

Very nice person but very bad teacher. He uses no technology in class, and writes everything in chalk with not so great handwriting. He is better for those who are comfortable with their calculus skills, but if you are learning calculus for the very first time I DO NOT RECOMMEND. He does not curve his hard tests which sets us up for failure.

analysis.name: Jade Fostvedt
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 91 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: One of, if not the best professor I've had in college. I learned a ton in this class and had fun doing so. This course was also not overly-demanding at all and I enjoyed it even though synthetic chemistry is not my field of interest within chemistry. Definitely take a class with Dr. Fostvedt if you can.

Best wet lab I've taken at UNC. To my knowledge, Dr. Fostvedt is the only lab instructor that actually releases the grading criteria used for evaluating each assignment. This course is 100x better than it used to be, and the pigment synthesis is actually pretty fun. Group research project at the end of the course with a poster presentation & paper.

Fostvedt is hands down the best chemistry professor at UNC. Shes so kind, good at teaching, and approachable. So reasonable with grades and classwork and takes feedback from students fullheartedly. An angel sent down to bring a little light to the hell that is the UNC chem department. If youre a chem major or need to take a chem class PLZ TAKE HER

Hands down my favorite chem professor so far (junior chem major). I love Dr Fostvedt so much. She cares about your learning, is kind, and is very accessible. She also wants you to succeed and do your best. Dr. Fostvedt will meet you where you are and try to help you every step of the way. Truly a wonderful human being--her kindness is contagious.

She is such a great person and great lecturer. She is open to feedback and is very passionate about her subject. By far the best chem prof I've had so far (chem major). She writes very fair exams and gives an extra credit question at the end of the midterms and final. There is also an opportunity to memorize the periodic table for a grade boost.

analysis.name: Rebecca Fish
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Fish is one of the most impressive and inspirational professors I've had. She cares deeply about each of her students and all of her assignments and exams are written to cause the least amount of confusion possible. Her exams are very fair and the few assignments we have are also not bad. She brings in cool guests lecturers sometimes too.

Prof. Fish is the reason why I decided to do the focus area in healthcare comms! Her lectures were SO interesting and tests were fair. She truly cares about her students learning what they need in class and in post grad. You actually learn content you'll use in life instead of doing meaningless busy work. I couldn't recommend her enough!!

Professor Fish is hands-down one of the best professors I've had at UNC. I took her for Healthcare Communications, and she made the class super engaging and actually taught skills that are super useful for getting a job. She really knows her stuff and genuinely cares about her students.

Prof. Fish is so caring and she really wants you to succeed! I recommend going to her Office Hours.
The tests go beyond her test review and you will be tested on some specific examples in her slide so make sure to go to class and pay attention to the details.

Prof. Fish is a great teacher and has tons of experience in the healthcare sector. Her lectures are interesting and she really cares about her students and wants them to succeed post grad. Her exams  only covered big topics which were on the slides and readings. If you listen in class and study a little for the exams, its an easy A!

analysis.name: Valerie Fields
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 40 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Straightforward class, one big group project that allowed us to apply what we learned, and we got to pick our groups or join a random one. 100 point quiz, 150 point project, 200 point final, 25 for research (filling out surveys), and 25 for attendance. These numbers get put together with the advertising section as well for the total grade.

I've had VK 3 times over the course of my time at Carolina and has been the most rewarding professor i've had. Her 137 course is completely different from her other two in where she grades in a tougher manner and expects a lot more from you, but it is 100% worth it. Provides strong portfolio materials and takes you out of your comfort zone.

Easy Mejo class with a quiz, exam, and one final PR portfolio project with same groups as AD section. Lectures were required and not that engaging, mainly based off posted slides. Quiz and final were similar and on Quizlet. Final project had clear rubric and she posted an example outline to follow. Didn't read the textbook at all.

VK is definitely an expert in her field! Her lectures are a bit dry but I also don't have much interest in PR so it might be that. The exam & quiz were easy, just straight from her notes. There's a pretty heavy group project, but as long as you're with a good group, it should be a breeze (also get to choose your own group).

Valerie knows what she is talking about, but the lectures are not that interesting. The creative assignment is done with the same groups are the advertising section, so just keep that in mind. Study for the quiz and final on quizlet. Reading the textbook isn't necessary.

analysis.name: Sebastian Filner
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Their lectures are quite dry and they don't explain the topics too well. I will say that they try their best and are accessible outside of class by email and in person. The tests are not too difficult but it ultimately depends on how you utilize the homeworks to study (do it!) and don't bother with the TAs (they're no help).

WORST TEACHER!  80% of your grade is the tests/final, and considering his inability to teach, the class average on almost every test was a D. We had to try to teach ourselves all the material (and theres alot). Despite low test scores he didn't seem to realize his teaching style was the problem.  Worst teacher ive ever had - spare yourself.

The majority of the class had significant trouble with this class because of the grading scale, where tests make up pretty much all of your grade. Lectures are confusing and his definitions seem redundant. The class average for tests is either a high D or low C and there is no test corrections or extra credit. Please. do not take him.

He sucks... all of his midterms have a D average, these exams are worth 50% of your final grade; he offers no extra credit or corrections. no one ever knows what he is talking about and he does not teach material that is aligned with the course requirements.

The semester started off really rough, but he progressed in his teaching a little over time. I did not learn anything from his lectures; basically taught myself everything. The notes he would write on the board were pretty useful tho.

analysis.name: Fei Yu
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Quite a bit of work, but she is very flexible and understanding as a teacher.

Fei is a very passionate teacher. She was always communicative and understanding, so long as you communicate first. The class was organized as one major group-project with various smaller projects and quizzes sprinkled throughout. I would've liked to see shake-ups in team composition so you could interact with more students.

DON'T TAKE 718 W/ YU! I have taken other UX classes and was very excited to learn more in this class. Instead, I felt like each week hours were wasted of my life. Class was so DULL! She was never helpful at office hours and she didn't give clear instructions on the HW. Also didn't hesitate to call ppl out in class. MEJO 581 with Ruel was better!

I have never rated a professor before but I felt like it had to be done for Professor Fei Yu. She is one of the most unorganized teachers I have ever had. She assigns a lot of little projects that have quick turn around so we never really have time to understand the material. I feel like I waste my time in her class and wish I had a different teach

I didn't learn much from 718 as the flipped classroom & virtual format puts the full burden of learning on the student. Class time was not utilized well. We'd sometimes use 45+ mins to talk logistics/rubrics & skim over actual content. I will say that Yu responds to emails/feedback readily, isn't a harsh grader, and does games on Zoom though.

analysis.name: Gregory Kable
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 204 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: I would die for this man. He is the nicest professor I have ever had. If you slightly pay attention in class and know the general plot of the plays and vocab you will get an A on the exams. Participants have many opportunities for extra credit through reading and answering mundane questions. Course journal is a free 100! He is very accommodating.

IF YOU ARE A FIRST YEAR HONORS STUDENT TAKE THIS CLASS!! super duper easy to get an A - literally just show up, submit the 3 assignments (only 3.), and give an ounce of effort when you do your scene panel. in class we just watched movies and read aloud scenes and chatted, but most ppl were just doing homework from other classes. highly recommend!

This class was probably the easiest I've ever taken, and Dr. Kable is so funny, caring, and supportive. Literally 75% of your grade comes from three journal entries that he grades super leniently on. The rest is participation and one "scene panel" that is a really simple acting project.

Professor Kable is genuinely interested in his students (he learned everyone's name in my 40-person class). I enjoyed his selection of plays, and most reading was done in class. He is funny, invested, and very professional.

Kable is such an amazing professor! You'll read a lot of plays, but as long as you show up to class, occasionally participate, and think about the material, it's an easy A. He's a generous grader and clearly knows a ton about the subject material. Take any and all of his classes!!

analysis.name: Prairie  Goodwin
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 119 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment:
Key takeaways so far:
1. answer keys frequently wrong
2. tests on content that is not taught during lecture
3. TA's are frequently incorrect during review sessions. Personally, I think its difficult to study when resources given are so obviously and drastically incorrect. Difficulty doesn't come from rigor of material, but rather these aspects.

The TAs don't know what they're doing but have large egos. Office hours are often a waste of time because most of the TAs are just as confused if not more confused than we are. The professor isn't receptive to feedback and lacks empathy. Tests are long and impossible to study for. Tests are also graded harshly. 

TLDR: Horrible in every aspect

This was the most discouraging class I've taken at Carolina. I felt completely unsupported and often left class feeling demoralized. The environment was unnecessarily harsh, and it seemed like the professor valued making things difficult over helping students learn. This experience made me seriously question pursuing this field.

The ratings speak for themselves. Don't come here rating her 5.0 just because you take 1-2 comp classes and a bunch of gen-eds, and have tons of time to go to office hours. The class isn't inclusive of people with really heavy schedules. Oh, and don't get confused by ratings from past semesters. Find a friend and compare how midterms have changed.

The professor doesn't teach how to write Java, making the class nearly impossible without prior experience, so you end up teaching it all to yourself. Exams are unlike reviews and worth 70% of the grade. Despite hours of studying, I feel set up to fail. I've always loved CS, but the lack of support and empathy makes this class deeply discouraging.

analysis.name: Govanni Granados
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Great professor! He explained complex topics in understandable ways, which reflected in high class averages for all exams. For reference, my midterm scores were 102 and 96, with final exam 100. No access to lectures outside of class, so attendance is necessary for course content, but really you just have to show up. Would absolutely take again!

He's a great guy and teaches very well, with great tests. Easy to get an A in the class if you do all your homework and study well. Lectures were worthwhile to go to, and online resources also taught in similar ways to this professor. Everyone should try to get him for MATH 381.

Govanni is really understanding. If the class doesn't understand a certain topic, he is willing to walk us through step by step. HW and Exams aren't too difficult if you study, and exams are easier than the HWs. For the final he let us bring in one sheet of notes. Class is graded by two midterms, weekly hws, and a final.

He was my favorite professor last semester. He is a great option if you want to actually learn the material AND want to get a good grade. He is willing to help answer questions for anything and clearly wants his students to succeed. His tests were very fair and went over material we had learned.

Really great lecturer, goes at a good pace and explains everything really clearly. Only downside is that his final grading scale was not very clear, nor were our final grades released, only saw our grade in Connect Carolina at the end.

analysis.name: Gary Kayye
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 160 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Gary is an awesome professor! Most importantly, his class projects, whether on New Media and technologies or  Branding of Me, actually make an impact on the students life as well as the community around us.
This class requires you to blog a lot. A lot. Did I say a lot?  But he's clear about it upfront. And, it works.  It is a individual branding class so.

I took Gary because he was highest on Rate my Professor and it was the best decision ever. His lectures were very interesting and he was a very real person. He told lots of jokes and showed videos that made it interesting. There was only two assignments were we had ample amount of time. You work with a partner so its super easy.

prof kayye is an interesting guy. he seems very different than most others in the J school as he wants you to want to learn the material rather than force feed it. you can certainly get by with just sitting in his lectures and making a great grade in the class but, you get out of his projects what you put into them.

Great professor who clearly cares about what he's teaching. You don't have to take any notes or attend lectures to pass. The final for his portion of the class is super straightforward. There is one or two group projects for his part of the class which aren't bad at all but the rubric is a little vague and he barely gives out 100s for the projects.

analysis.name: Michelle Gravatt
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 35 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I have a hard time understanding her other reviews. She is kind and tries to make class fun, and while you do have to participate often you have chances to volunteer yourself before being called on. It may feel a little more difficult if you haven't taken Spanish in a few years, but otherwise she gives very helpful study guides to do well on exams.

Seems sweet in class but grades harshly on basic assignments and exams. Cold calls consistently in class so pay attention, participation matters and is not an easy grade. Exams are 45% of grade and are taken in class on zoom. If you are looking for an easy grade, this class is not it. If I could go back I'd take this at community college instead.

I'm not taking Spanish at UNC again. Class only meets on Thursdays & you have groupstudy on Tuesdays. You teach yourself the material and quickly review it in class. She is not easy to reach via email, which isn't great as this class is online. There is an online textbook and all of the homework is through Pearson. Fairly easy verbal grader though.

Maybe she's super passionate about the material she's teaching - I don't know. But she is very condescending and teaches like we're in middle school. When I got questions wrong in class (she randomly calls people every 2 minutes), she would make comments in front of the whole class that my audio must be broken on my laptop or mock the answer.

Prof Gravatt is very snarky and not understanding of personal conflicts whatsoever. Her lectures are not helpful at all because we often go over entire chapters in 50 minutes. Midterms have content we've never seen before yet are somehow expected to know. I would avoid taking this course with her if possible.

analysis.name: Harry LLoyd
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Harry is new to the United States, but he is a kind, funny, and passionate instructor. He is understanding with his students, perhaps due to being a younger professor, and he tries hard to be friendly and engaging. There's a lot of reading, but you can get through it. Make sure to participate in class. He is a hard grader, but not unfair
He's a really great professor. He's pretty funny which makes the class enjoyable. He's obviously really knowledgeable and skilled at what he does, but still very modest and approachable.
Phenomenal professor, take his class if you get a chance

analysis.name: Herica Valladares
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 47 reviews — fairly reliable
