analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Takes attendance randomly. 30% of your grade comes from the group research project, and groups are randomly assigned. Willing to help and gives extensions. The first exam was a little hard, but we were allowed a cheat sheet. The final was open note. She does grade the group project pretty strictly, so follow the rubric closely. Great prof!
I would take 273 with her if you can—great professor, clear expectations, and straightforward grading.

She is a good person but the class sucks. Its a lot of material that she does not really explain well. She will answer questions about it after though. She does allow extensions on a lot of the assignments. She is a tough grader especially on the one big project that is spread out over the semester. She is REALLY SPECIFIC about the grading for it

Dr. Kucera is an awesome professor who truly wants her students to succeed! She is very flexible with deadlines. Your experience in this class will HEAVILY depend on the group you are randomly assigned to work with for the semester (as this is a huge project worth a huge chunk of your grade). Ask questions when you're confused and you'll be fine.

Awesome, kind and very helpful professor. I met with her many times during office hours to get help with the group project. If you are assigned a good group then you'll be fine in this class. The first exam was a little difficult but we did get a cheat sheet. Second exam was take home. Took attendance one time the day before spring break.

analysis.name: Kyle Cessna
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great professor who is passionate about material and intent on helping his students learn. Students could turn essays in again after reviewing feedback which was extremely helpful. Occasionally a little confusing with class schedule or due dates, but would always correct it and ensure everything was fair for students.

Mr. Cessna is very passionate about philosophy and managed to make a dry course relatively entertaining. You can tell he really cares about students and wants to see people succeed. His grading criteria is very clear and he provides constructive feedback on submissions.

I only took this course because of the major requirement but thought Kyle was a great teacher. I am not super into philosophy and wouldn't have taken this if it wasn't required, but Kyle had nothing to do with that. He is accessible and willing to work with you if you are with him. The grading system was slightly confusing but overall good prof.

analysis.name: Katherine Turk
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 53 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I didn't mind prof. Turk's lectures, although there is little to no information on the powerpoint slides so you really have to do your best taking notes. the main thing is that the TA grades every assignment in this course (final too). Even though the recitations were decent, it's like she refused to grade anything 100 (ex-4.8/5 participation)

great lectures, but they were scripted. accessible outside of class in office hours, but the TAs were the ones responsible for your entire grade. 3 short papers, midterm paper, participation, and a final comprised your entire grade; people complained about their grades from TA but I think they were fair and expected college level writing.

She reads lectures from a script in an immersive, storytelling style. Barely any details on slideshows, so prepare to listen and take notes. TAs do the grading for the essays you write. It feels like the grading criteria were not clear. Prepare for boring essay prompts. The lectures are 2 days a week, and the recitation is once a week.

Material was simple and interesting, but lectures were read off a script. Prof. Turk isn't very involved outside of lecture. The reason the class was more difficult was due to the TAs. TAs consistently gave specific and harsh feedback, making it hard to do well on the 5 essays we were graded on. Recitation participation was the only other grade.

Turk is a good professor and accesible for office hours. Lectures were interesting as well as content. Although all of the assignmnets were short papers with such broad and confusing prompts. It felt like even though I knew exactly what the content we were learning was, my grade wasnt reflected because the essays were so difficult.

analysis.name: Karl Castillo
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Total of 4(?) group projects, he keeps randomly adding them. Forgets to unlock canvas files. Doesn't answer emails. Exams have niche questions. Questions are worded poorly. Cold calls in class. Lectures are boring. With COIL, this class feels like 2 classes worth of work. Loved him for 490; I dread attending 442. Quiz every class. I'm frustrated.

Bio 457/EMES 442. Long tangent filled lectures. Super unclear exams, that aren't application based & are random facts from one-off slides. This class was sooo easy but when it came to exam time, people lost a lot of points because of how stupid the questions were. Would not take a class from him again.

Dr. Castillo is a passionate speaker. He is insightful and holds office hours and will work to make sure his class succeeds. I have taken 4 classes with him so far and plan to take more! There are also extra credit opportunities if you go to class.
This class was about corals and it was so interesting. There is a project, but the topic can be chosen for wahtever you want. As long as you try, you will succeed.

The professor is very lecture heavy, which are not very clear, and always gives pop quizzes. He has a very intricate and unclear grading system. Throughout the semester he promises that if you do all the extra credit you will be moved up a letter grade but after the final exam he claimed that no extra credit will be given or contribute to grades.

analysis.name: Xinyun Liu
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Liu is a very great lecturer who explains thing pretty well. His midterms and individual part for the final exam are easy. The common part of final exam is kinda challenging... but Dr. Liu's easy individual part really makes up for my bad grade in the common part. He is very accessible and really cares for the students. HIGHLY RECOMMEND!!!

Professor Liu was great. I loved watching him get progressively covered in chalk throughout the lectures. 

Seriously though, Professor Liu is a great guy and his lectures cover enough content so reading the textbook is unnecessary. My only note is that there can sometimes be a slight language barrier when asking questions.

Professor Liu is very caring and understanding. I struggled a bit for the exams but if you show up to class, pay attention, and start studying early you'll be fine. 
His lectures are great and really clear, and you can tell he cares about the subject and the class. Great professor, choose him!

Dr. Liu is a GREAT prof. He rescheduled two exams to fit classes needs, includes extra credit, and has great lectures. He is hilarious, and very good at explaining concepts. Highly recommend taking. If you pay attention, you will get an A.

Xinyun is a good time! Never read the textbook and was able to get all necessary content from lecture which typically ended early for time to ask questions about HW (or eat lunch early lol). He's funny in class and cares a lot. I can't recommend him more.

analysis.name: Saman Lagzi
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Lagzi is awesome. Take him if you can. He is very passionate about what he teaches and truly wants to see the students succeed. Very clear slides and lectures. He answers his email in seconds and is very reasonable when it comes to giving you points back on exams. Great teacher but most importantly a great person.

Professor Lagzi is great. 403 is outside of my focus and Lagzi made it really straightforward and accessible. The exams are tough but if you build templates before you should be in a good spot. The group project is very important so make sure to spend real time on it. Homeworks were generally easy and Professor Lagzi cares about his students.

Professor Lagzi is incredibly caring. Many homework assignments that can take you hours to do, but it's pretty standardized across diff 403 classes. 2 midterms that were both hard and a time crunch, but the final is easier in comparison, all open note. Content gets easier later on. Group project takes a lot of time, but grading criteria is clear.

Saman is a great professor who cares about his students. I did not do as well in his class as I would have liked, but that's definitely on me. There's a big group project, homework might take a few hours, and exams are a little time-crunched. A lot of work is on Excel. Definitely pay attention during lecture and take good notes!

analysis.name: Laura Ott
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 320 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Refuses to provide a copy of lecture slides, so half the class is scrambling to write down content instead of listening to what she's saying. Exam questions do not test on knowledge of content but instead are written to trick us. Median on the first midterm was a 74 AFTER 10 bonus points were added and 7 questions dropped (out of 27). Dr. Opp fr

Dr. Ott wants you to think she cares. However, that is only true for a select few. Very passive aggressive, not willing to answer questions, not widely accessible outside class, and relies heavily on peer mentors. Beware of "select all" questions with no partial credit and no low stakes practice for tests. Dr. Ott? More like "Dr. Opp."

Dr. Ott is one of the most condescending professors that I have had the unfortunate displeasure of taking a class with. The information of the class was bearable but whenever you need something from her, it always seems like she is talking down on you. If you have the chance to take a class with her, do yourself a favor and don't.

The course content itself is not hard to understand as a whole. However, Dr. Ott teaches the concepts in a nonlinear way that is hard to understand if you don't reorder it yourself. GRQs were a waste of time and lectures could be confusing at times. Pay attention to the lecture slides when studying. She is getting rid of cheat sheets soon - beware.

Does not seem to care about students. Extremely condescending, makes the class more difficult then it needs to be and also takes her bad mood out on students. Content is not difficult, exams are not necessarily hard but they are clearly meant to trick you rather than assess your actual understanding. Voice is really annoying.

analysis.name: Lillian Zwemer
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 201 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Dr Z has some really hard exams and doesn't release them afterwards. She talks to you like a kindergartener and her slides are really convoluted. Good enough lecturer, but she doesn't annotate her slides much. The course is heavily credited on exams, and there are a lot of homework assignments that do not always feel relevant.
Zwemer is super nice, and 220 really isn't that bad of a class, ESPECIALLY if you take it after taking 103 with Ott.  Zwemer's exams are much more clear and about Biology than Ott's.

The content itself is easy, but her wording on exams is what will get you. Claims she has spent years studying people and knows what works best, but also constantly has errors in exams. INCREDIBLY passive aggressive and condescending when you ask questions. This class would've been so easy if I had taken it with someone else. Do not recommend.

She messes up at least one part of the lecture in every class, and she requires both in/outside participation. I also disliked how she does not release her actual exams, just scores. Instead, she schedules two sessions to 'view' your exam, which I have never been able to go to due to class/work conflicts. Avoid her class at all costs.

Fine person but a terrible lecturer. Her lectures are disorganized, and you can never tell what is going to be on a test or not. Because of that, her tests are also absolutely atrocious to take. Overall, she's very nice, but her class is so disorganized and poorly taught that I just never want her again as a professor.

analysis.name: Matthew Lockett
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 81 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I waited a semester to take this course with Lockett because of his amazing reviews but I was incredibly disappointed. Expect to go over the easiest question in class 4 times only to open the HW to the most impossible unrelated questions. All the tests, including the final, are open note but he makes them very difficult.

He gives you all the tools you need to succeed, on top of some amazingly inspirational speeches at the beginning and throughout the course. Quite literally he has made me cry from how much he cares for his students. Absolute best chemistry teacher at UNC.

Best professor I've ever had. His exams are hard, but he prepares you for them by giving you tons of practice exams. I could have failed every exam of his and would still rate him like this. Funny lecturer, understanding teacher, and overall great person. If you do bad on an exam DON'T PANIC because you always have the chance to replace it

Worst class ever!!! Everyone told me that he was a good professor, however he did not live up to my expectations. I genuinely don't understand how so many students like Lockett. Lectures were disorganized and didn't help with understanding the content. Half of his answer keys are incorrect. Tests are extremely difficult!!

Was so excited that I got into his class because of his 5.0 ratings but wish I took a different professor. Did not like this class, tests were hard and much different from the in class practice. He would say the test was going to be easier than practice exams and then it would be much harder.

analysis.name: Monica Lopez Vazquez
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I loved her so much, I am pretty sure any exams/assignments are not created by her but by the department she just grades them but they are relatively easy. Class participation is a grade and she cold calls a lot but is forgiving if you dont know it. Highly recommend!

Loved Monica, can safely say that she can write me a recommendation letter in the future! She is so kind and made me happy to walk into her class every single day; I could not have made this first semester without her.

She made what could have been a boring grammar pre-req fun and she pushed us a lot. Definitely an amazing Spanish professor. Tests were tough but she gave us a huge extra credit at the end which helped a lot.

Very very nice and helpful. She genuinely cared about you and your grade and was accommodating. She was not intimidating and would help if you were struggling. Try to do most of the readings before each class though so you don't get lost.
Monica is a great Spanish teacher. She's super nice, open to questions, and helps you with mistakes instead of looking down on you for them. Make sure you come to class prepared though.

analysis.name: Michal Osterweil
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 77 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Dr. O is odd, but I do recommend her because she'll change the way you look at the world forever. This class can be frustrating at times in the way that's like those tiktoks where the girl is like "when a man has a thought I had when I was 7," but don't take it to heart if she comes off as patronizing because she really does just want you to learn.

Dr. O is a genuinely good person who cares about the class, her students, and the content. This being said, the class is semi-disorganized and can be redundant. Lots of reading, but you only really need to do it if you're doing the reading responses. Pretty liberal and not very open to other perspectives, so prepare accordingly. Go to office hours!

Dr. O is a great professor! The class itself was honestly not too bad, you just have to keep up with some readings (and you can honestly get away with not doing a lot of them). The tests were mostly short response with some multiple choice, but Dr. O and the TAs grade pretty leniently. Overall, fun and straightforward class!

We meditated before each class, which was kind of cool in my opinion. The course material is interesting and progressive, if you're into that stuff. The papers and exams were graded very leniently. If you can showcase within them that you did the readings / kept up with class, you will do very well. Attendance is not mandatory, but I recommend it.

Dr. Osterweil's class was definitely the most unique I've ever taken really - it is obvious she cares about the material very deeply and really gets you thinking. Even though the class could've been a bit more organized and straightforward, I really enjoyed the energy and knowledge she brought to the course. She's also very kind, open, and driven.

analysis.name: Mariana Olvera-Cravioto
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: It's undeniable she is a great person passionate about the subject and accessible outside the classroom. However I would not recommend taking this if you don't have background in stats or math. The lectures are extremely confusing and require you to know the material beforehand. Hardest tests ever. If you take 435 with her good luck you'll need it.

Lower averages than other sections. Failed several of her students. Insufficient prep materials for the exams. No recorded lectures. Exams were much more challenging than other sections. Homeworks take a ridiculous amount of time to complete. Professor has been falsely represented in her ratings. You've been warned. No curves when over half fail...

I had her for 435 and 445. I really struggled through 435 and her lectures started getting pretty confusing, but shes still an amazing prof and person. I'm half way through 445 right now and I do think it's a lot easier than 435 and I am enjoying my second class with her a lot more than my first.

To be honest I did not know what to expect with a 400 level class called Introduction to Probability but it was like two weeks of introduction. PSA that you need to do calculus in this course and I spent like 14 hours a week on this class. Go to class and do extra practice problems because honestly understanding the question is half the battle.

This was by far the hardest class I have ever taken. The exams were very hard and you needed to study for two weeks to just pass the exams. Mariana however is accessible outside of class and wants to help. Just know if you plan on taking this course, buckle up. Originally, I had a D but my final grade was "curved" to a B so that slayed.

analysis.name: Maharshi Vyas
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Vyas is a really cool guy overall. His lectures are interesting and it's clear that he is very knowledgable about the subjects. I took this class for an FC and it actually expanded my POV on a subject I wouldn't have otherwise studies. Prof. Vyas is great and this class is not stressful, a nice break from harder classes. 100% take again.

He is one of my favorite professors I've ever had! He's a super kind person and very clearly extremely knowledgeable on what he is teaching, and passionate about it. However, he still gives students ample time to discuss topics on their own, which is probably my favorite aspect of the class. His class Reli 388  isn't very work heavy either!

Honestly an amazing professor. One in class test that is graded well and showcases what one truly took from the class material. He is extremely accessible outside of class and so caring. Truly knows the content and welcomes differing ideas and opinions. I recommend taking his course(s) and if I can, I will again.

M. Vyas is a great professor! Super engaging during office hours and cares about his students ability to succeed. Had a small class cohort this semester but he's looking for new students to join and I would def encourage you to do so! His RELI 388 class has FC Global/Ethics and there's only one SHORT midterm exam. The rest is fun projects, etc.

If you are interested in learning more about Hinduism and Hindus as a diaspora I recommend RELI 281. We learned many interesting things about their history/religion/culture and how communities adapt to new settings. Prof. Maharshi is very nice, a fair grader, and all you need to do is read the material and participate to earn a good grade.

analysis.name: Miriam Watts
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Love Dr. Watts! She provides all her lectures on videos in case you ever missed information or want extra review. It is important to go to class due to graded participation assignments. She makes information really easy to understand though so just study hard for the tests because that is mainly what your overall grade is based off of.

The concepts are hard,but she teaches them in a way that make sense and her slides are very organized. I never read the textbook because she posts video lectures,and her tests are mainly on the stuff covered in class. There is only 1 assignment a week, 3 tests + final, and 4 group assignments. Dr. Watts is an amazing professor, take her if you can!

Awesome professor; really good at explaining complex topics and doesn't make her tests any harder than they needed to be. If you study the content, you'll do fine on the tests (which are all MCQ). The first unit is the easiest because it just focuses on neuroanatomy (the other units combine multiple body systems). Group project case studies are ok.

Without a doubt, Dr. Watts is the best STEM prof I've ever had. Her slides actually made sense (unlike so many other classes), she posts recorded lectures, and the exam questions are the fairest questions I've literally ever seen in my life. If you study for this class, you will do well on exams - the way God intended (even tho it's rare for UNC).

The content is moderately hard, and there is a lot of it, but she lectures very clearly and is engaging! The powerpoints and learning objectives have everything you need to know for exams, which are honestly very fair. Homework and other assignments aren't overly tedious. The only thing is that the polleverywheres are graded for accuracy.

analysis.name: Markus Nevil
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: In my opinion, this class needs to be entirely restructured. Very odd grading system, tons of group work, and zero real learning. Also, zero accountability for group members not helping. Basically, a high school science class. Despite Bio 101 being a pre-rec, we spend time in 105L learning 101L skills. Could be useful, just needs to be rethought.

Idk why Bio105L gets the bad rep. This class was super easy GPA booster. It's the easiest bio class I've ever took. The assignments didn't even take that long either. The only things that took time were the papers. But then again, they were group assignments and u r only responsible for one or two sections. Easy class. No final exam, just a paper.

Nevil seems great, but this class is the worst. Unsure if he set it up or was assigned to teach it, but it's the most nonsense class you'll ever take. Assignments take forever, are pointless, and have insanely confusing rubrics; it's a busy work highschool class. I don't get how this is considered a prereq because holy yap and time/energy wasted.

Dr. Nevil was great in 105. There was a lot of work to do every week but there were a lot of interesting topics. Due dates were clear and easy to keep track of. The rubrics for the projects were helpful and you can resubmit if you dont do well on the first try. Dr. Nevil was the the professor who did the lectures and I thought they were interesting

Dr. Nevil is kind, but the lectures are a waste of time. No exams, just a bunch of projects/assignments. Peer Reviews, discussion boards, and easy online video quizzes. Every week, there's a group lab report assignment. Annoying but easy once you get the hang of it. This class had so many assignments, but easy A. Good GPA boost

analysis.name: Mike O’Sullivan
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. O'Sullivan is my favorite lecturer at UNC. He is passionate and pretty funny, although self-deprecating at times. I liked learning how history is still very relevant today in stuff like politics. Few assignments, and most are completion/effort. There is a lot of reading, but take the time to go thru everything to ace the midterm and final.

Professor O'Sullivan gave detailed lectures and made sure to show every side of the various historical debates that we covered throughout the course. He provides good feedback on writing for history and is willing to talk and answer questions outside of class in general. He is very smart but also very down-to-earth, and kind to students.

Heavy on the reading, but very entertaining lectures, talks as if he is telling a story and is easy to learn from. Easily accessible outside class, very kind and very understanding. Encourages people with no outside knowledge to take the class. Highly recommend.

Hard to take notes on his lectures, it's a LOT in a short class period. Very story telling adjacent, so he's fun to listen to if you enjoy the topics. Amusing, very caring, chill professor. Very understanding. Graded on midterm paper and final paper, and a few reader response. Lots of reading and one group presentation.

Professor O'Sullivan is so nice! The course is easy overall: 3 reader responses, a map quiz, a mid-term, and a final exam. Don't stress if you are struggling, go to his office hours and he's always willing to help. This course is reading heavy and quite fast paced, so make sure you do your readings or else you'll feel lost in class

analysis.name: Zachary Nimchuk
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He is super nice and so interested in CRISPR! There is 1 midterm and you get a cheat sheet. The average was a B+ and he gave us 2 questions back that people missed. Otherwise, the only other grades are a semester long group project and the "final exam" which is a prompt that you get ahead of time and can prepare. Definitely recommend this class.

Dr. Nimchuk has been by far the best biology professor I've ever had at UNC. This course was my absolute favorite this semester and I always looked forward to sitting in lecture. He is very knowledgable with the CRISPR-CAS9 system and has so much insight to provide. Everyone should take this class and witness how marvelous Dr. Nimchuk is!

The best professor I've had at UNC. Very clear on his expectations and willing to explain difficult concepts. The class material was interesting for people interested in genetics and molecular biology. If you read and understand the papers, you will do well.

Loved Dr. Z! His lectures were engaging and entertaining to learn from. He sent out a weekly email that outlined the class we had that week and showed how much he cared about us. This was a 1-credit seminar, but he's teaching a full course on CRISPR next semester which I would highly recommend!

Great professor and person. Very knowledgeable on the subject matter and cares about the students. Seems as though he wants students to succeed and learn from his class. I highly recommend his course on CRISPR if you're a science major and interested in genetics. Final project was a lot of fun.

analysis.name: Natasha Parikh
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 128 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: super nice, class was rly easy, poll everywhere everyday, labs every week for completion, assignments every week for accuracy but super easy and using jamovi. take home quizzes, midterm and final. uses piazza for participation

She was wonderful. The course was structured where you watch lectures before class and do some compression questions (but that should take about an hour at most). In class we do review, poll everywhere, and work on assignments, which is really nice because you can get a lot of help from both Dr Parikh and the ULAs/IAs. Overall a great course!!!

Amazing Professor! She made retaking stats an enjoyable experience. The one downside I had with her is that she often would respond to emails very late, and many times she wouldn't even respond. But, it was not an issue as she was very welcoming and always had time before or after class to speak. I would recommend taking a class with her!

She is a great person! But on top of that this is the best class I have taken. The lectures are actually interesting and all test are take home and open note including the midterm and final. The only thing that you are not guaranteed to get an 100 on is the in class assignment but those don't really impact your grade. Take this easiest A.

Prof. Parikh is a great professor! 210 content was a bit dull just because it's statistics focused, but she made it as interesting as it can be. Easy A! Labs are automatic 100s as long as you try, and assignments are pretty easy. I liked how detailed instructions were provided. Overall, take it with Prof. Parikh.

analysis.name: John Noonan
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Noonan is a truly a kind person, making an effort to remember your name and make 231 really entertaining than most sections. But most of us agree that he tends to ramble about things that aren't important to the curriculum at hand. the ULAs in recitation were def better at teaching than he was in general. also poll everywhere is used for attendance

Dr. Noonan is lots of fun! Teaching style could definitely be improved, but he's engaging and silly, and answers specific questions pretty well. I leaned HEAVILY on the ULAs in the class to actual learn material and prepare for tests... probably would've failed without them. Overall fun, but you're left to learn a lot of material on your own.

Professor Noonan is a great example of great person, somewhat lackluster teacher imo. He is lively and brings good energy to his classroom. However, his teaching is not my cup of tea. The material that is on exams is very different from what's practiced on homework/class. It was often "figure it out" with not much else for help. I felt unprepared.

Professor Noonan was such a great person and I enjoyed having him but the class itself was not the best. He was very all over the place and felt like we didn't have enough time to do anything. He is new though, so I think he's just getting the hang of thing and hopefully future classes will be more organized. I had to teach myself most material :(

I really like Noonan as a person and he wants the best for his students, his only down fall is the lectures. He gives a worksheet to work through and just gives the answers at the end of class. This was his first semester teaching here, so he was figuring out what worked along the way, so hopefully future classes will have more productive lectures.

analysis.name: Nora Hanagan
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 28 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Prof Hanagan is very knowledgeable and passionate about the subject matter, and she heavily encourages participation. She's a relatively tough grader as far as first year seminars go, but if you do the readings and put in some effort, you should be fine. The major grades are an in-class debate, short video, two reading analyses, and an oral exam.

Awesome professor, not too difficult. HEAVY readings and has textbooks, but can probably get PDFs. Main grade is a debate which needs a fair amount of effort but she gives class time, groups are random but worked out pretty well for our class. Fair grader, just put effort into your papers and reflections and in class writings and you'll do fine.

I liked this class. Lots of reading some required for in class assignments. She's a great lecturer so it made a class I was worried about more digestible. There's a debate at the end of the semester you might be worried about but it was pretty easy. Not a harsh grader either

I like her a lot, she is very sweet and caring. Flexible with attendance if you have a genuine reason you can't be there. her lectures are sometimes quite dry. Class involves a lot of group discussion so do the readings.

Dr. Hanagan is incredibly knowledgeable about American political theory and delivers lectures in a way that make the content digestible. There is a significant amount of content covered, so I would recommend doing all the readings if you want an A. She is accessible and helpful in office hours. Overall, I learned a lot and improved as a writer.

analysis.name: Ali Nezhad
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Currently taking for STOR415 (not listed on ratemyprofessor). analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Quinones-Ayala was an absolute joy to have as a professor. So caring, enthusiastic, and engaging with her students. Spanish 203 is a hard class, but she was very encouraging if you ever made mistakes and would explain them in a very understandable way. Some of her grading took a long time but I can't even care because of how amazing she was.

Funny and bubbly- Brenda is my favorite out of all instructors I've had thus far at UNC.  She's caring, passionate, personable, and truly understands the Spanish language (as she is a native Puerto Rican.)   I went to office hours for help on a paper & she not only worked through my paper with me, but also asked about my interests and future plans.

Dr. QA was extremely kind, caring, enthusiastic, understanding, and accommodating. I am not the best Spanish speaker but was able to get through this class because I made an effort. If you care, you will do fine because she cares! A great option (if not one of the best) for 203.

Genuinely one of the best professors I have encountered during my time at UNC. Take her class if you can, while SPAN203 can be difficult, she will do anything to support you and make learning Spanish fun and accessible.

Professor Quinones-Ayala was my favorite spanish teacher of all time! She was hilarious and generally cared about her students. I felt seen and competent, even when I completely messed stuff up as is bound to happen in a language class. She did the absolute best she possibly could with the freedom she was given, and I am so grateful.

analysis.name: Richard Rimanyi
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 96 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Rimanyi is a great professor. I really enjoyed number theory with him. When signing up for classes, I skim to see what he and few others teaches first and then look at course titles. He covers a lot of material but at what feels like a very comfortable pace. I always feel confident that he won't lose me in lecture.

One of the best professors in MATH I've had during my time at UNC. Amazing lectures, clear explanations and fair tests. His lectures are extremely worth to attend. I would definitely take another course with him if I have the chance.
Definitely take linear with Rimanyi! His tests aren't made to trick you, so just study the homework and you'll be fine.
He's a really great lecturer--super clear. I had him for linear algebra, and he's the reason I got through it okay. His tests are difficult but really fair; there were never any tricks.
Although 521 is considered as kind of a hard class, he really made it interesting, exciting and relatively easy to get a high grade

analysis.name: Richard Superfine
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Super awesome guy! Took him for APPL 60, and you can tell this guy really cares about his students. He invited the entire class to his house for a pizza party, and so he could talk about his woodworking. Class is mainly based around 2 papers (1 based on a gift that you build and the second a research paper), and making a GIF of a tree.

Rich is super fun and a really good teacher for this class. While lectures could sometimes be a drag, the class was generally engaging and fun. He made us all cakes when the Eagles won the Superbowl. Knows a lot about designing and solving problems. Loves answering questions and helping you solve problems. Definitely recommend this class and Rich!
Rich is super chill, funny, and knowledgeable about woodworking. There were very few assignments, but the grading was easy and they were typically quite fun.

So this professor loves to be inspirational and is so sweet. Sometimes he gets passionate about things that few care deeply about, but I think that is a bit of the nature of the course. One can only have so much interest in different types of fasteners. Classes are graded by undergrad TA's of whom are easy graders.

He cares about what he teaches. It might be a bit of work, but it's not that bad, especially if you're interested in design and making. This is a fun class and a super easy A if you do the work. Some people were tripping about the grading, which could be unclear at times, but everyone got an A in that class.

analysis.name: Rosa Li
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 90 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: There are weekly mini quizzes that are open note and pretty easy, and the group activities, almost every week, are also easy. Other regular lecture days are lowk boring, but not bad. They are a nice break from the other days imo. You need to know textbook terms for the exams, but you get a clear study guide and cheat sheet!

Rosa Li is generally a nice person, but her exams are unnecessarily confusing. Many of the questions are worded in an unclear and overly complicated way, and it often feels intentional. The difficulty of the class comes more from how questions are phrased than from the actual material. I'd avoid unless required.

Dr. Li was super helpful and very accommodating to all students.  Class has 2 midterms and a final (non-cumulative), 2 papers (short like 1-2 pages) and a group project (easy), lots of extra credit opportunities so you would genuinely have to try to do poorly in the class.

Clear criteria with fair grading (you picked one of two grading schemes). Your grade is based on attendance, pre-class readings (if you picked the scheme with those), 3 tests, 2 papers, and the final. Classroom discussions were fun, and the material was interesting! I thought it was a relatively easy course, just pay attention to the slides.

Dr. Li was extremely fair. Students could pick how they want to be graded based on strengths. Her exams were not super easy, but definitely not hard.They required studying because she would ask very specific questions. However, all her test questions were reflected on the slides. Definitely recommend this class, even though lectures can be boring.

analysis.name: Yaron Shemer
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 22 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: If you're taking it for the Gen-Ed, be aware that it's a decent amount of work, & unless you're really interested in film, you probably won't enjoy it. Dr. Shemer is very caring and a generous grader, but lectures can be mind-numbing. If you do the readings, watch the films, and participate, you WILL get an A, but don't expect a fun class.

Dr. Shemer's lectures are interesting. If you don't know a lot about Jerusalem, sometimes it can be difficult to follow some of the concepts he talks about, but he's always willing to answer questions and clarify when needed. Great person!

Seems like a nice guy but I dreaded going to class. Drones on and on and I had a hard time following. On tests and essays he wanted me to parrot back what he taught, didn't appreciate me putting in my own perspective on things. A deep dive into Middle Eastern Cinema, but I came out of it still not understanding why that was important.

Assigns ridiculous amounts of reading, especially for a 100 level class. I've been in college for years and have never had to do this much reading. He is a very good lecturer and encourages his students to think and discuss the themes found in the reading, though you will mostly be discussing that at recitation if you're in a large lecture class.

Yaron is a great guy and is clearly passionate about the subject matter. Attendance is mandatory and participation is recommended since lectures are discussion based. Reading for every class that is a bit dry but I would recommend doing it since he asks for opinions. 4-5 papers graded fairly easily. Straight-forward final. Some extra credit.

analysis.name: William Snider
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Dr Snider is really caring, his lectures are great, and provides good slides. WA helped me understand the content though they can be time consuming. I wouldn't take the class if I didn't have the time to study for it, but the content is super interesting. He likes questions at the end of class, feedback from students and is open to improvements.

Dr. Snider is great - he genuinely cares about getting to know his students. The course material is super relevant to emerging research in neuroscience but is challenging. The tests are most
of the grade and are detailed, but are very fair and if you study the powerpoints you'll do fine. Attendance isn't scored but participation in discussions is

If you want to continue to pursue the field of neuroscience or psychology, do NOT take this professor. He only gives you lecture slides to study, nothing else, and he doesn't let you see review past exams at all. The class is supposed to be PRINCIPLES of brain circuits and he expects you to know every intricacy of every circuit. I despise this man.

Dr. Snider is very knowledgeable and covers many aspects of the systems neuroscience field in an up-to-date way which I loved. The exams were fairly written. He often speeds through lecture slides so be prepared to review after class. I would recommend taking this class if you're looking for a challenging but rewarding class.

Currently as a research tech in neurobiology, I think this class was the most useful class in undergrad. If you want to do any type of wet lab neuroscience research such a great course to do so. Yes the class is hard, but it's by far the most relevant and up to date class I've taken that teaches you about systems neuroscience.

analysis.name: Scott Warren
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 52 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I seldom post on RateMyProfessor. But Prof. Warren deserves this. He is inspirational. Period. Does an exemplary job of breaking down those tough acid-base questions. He's also very friendly. After all, it's a Chem class at UNC, so be ready to read. But Dr. Warren is always there for you. To me, he's a Chem god. Reason why I'm now a Chem major.

He's so passionate about chem and has a molecule of the day, that said the chem department is evil so you probably won't do too well on exams. Lecture can get boring but I think that's honestly any chem lecture. Use your resources.

As an otherwise straight-A student, this class butchered my mental health. The tests didn't reflect the class content, which I thought I understood; I did well on daily quizzes but not on tests. Warren was best choice of 3 profs, but department is bad, no curves or EC. I hope no one ever has to take it online again. Ended w/ a 79.3... so annoying!

He messed up our final grades and stressed an entire class of student out telling us our grades were wrong. To add, the test average was terrible and the class in general is a risk to take. Its not even a hit or miss with this professor, it is simply a miss. You will do poorly and that is a promise. Good luck :)

I don't post on RMP often, but I think Dr. Warren deserves it because he is a professor who will listen to you and readily answers your questions even outside of class. He once stayed on for a whole hour after class to explain chemistry concepts to me and a few other students. Therefore, I think you will have a better chance of succeeding with him.

analysis.name: Saiful Tamim
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Wonderful professor, gives clear explanation for all concepts. Cares about his students, very accommodating. Class is not difficult if you engage with the material, do the homework, and practice problems. There is nothing on exams that is not covered in class; very straightforward, I got a 100% on every exam. Lowest exam grade replaced w/final.

Saiful is a great lecturer and teaches 383 very well. It can be fast if you aren't well-rooted in previous math classes, but it is still doable. He is nice, pretty funny, and considerate. It is best to go over lecture notes and provided worksheets to study for his midterms and finals. The tests are hard and worth a lot of your grade, study.

Lectures are a little fast and he assumes you know stuff that he hasn't taught often. Seems like he would be a great prof for higher level math but not the best for the basics. Exams are very fair and cover the same kinds of problems that we did in class. Lowest midterm gets replaced by the final.

Great for a math prof. Got an A. Let us replace our lowest test score with our final if it was higher. TONS of resources available for outside of class (i.e. practice problems, so if you do all of the practice you'll do well on exams). Never puts anything on the exams that we haven't covered in class with practice problems or in previous exams.

Tamim is a very genuine professor and his class is pretty manageable even with a hard course load. His exams are doable but do require some studying if you want to do well. Highly recommend him for 383.

analysis.name: Tia James
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very kind and well-respected within the department. The class is fun and I learned a lot. Tia is hilarious

This is by far one of the best DRAM classes that I have taken at UNC. Tia was kind, understanding, and not to mention incredibly talented. I equate her to the raise your yayaya lady because she is a magic worker with her notes and direction. Would 10000% take again.

Such an amazing artist, performer, and mentor. She only asks that you come to class and put forth the effort, so there's not that much required of you time-wise, but you will learn so much! I left this class feeling fulfilled and with skills that I will always remember. Tia is wonderful.

Very passionate about Voice and Speech and an experienced Broadway actor who really wants her students to go for it. All grading is based off of your performances in class and three small (1-page) papers due at the end of the semester. Highly recommend for anyone passionate about acting, and looking for how to start using their voice fully!
Literally the best professor and mentor. Tia is so caring and creates a classroom that feels like a family. I learned and grew so much in this class. One of my favorite classes at UNC by far.

analysis.name: Teressa Bergland
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 99 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: As others have said, her exams are quite long.  However, she has recognized that the exams weren't completely fair and offered revisions worth half credit back.  Her lectures are pretty good and she's very accessible via office hours.  Homework is pretty light.  She's not perfect but def way better than some  of the other stor profs here.

Rated her 2 (not 1) bc she does genuinely have a passion and it shows in her good lectures. HOWEVER, she is not meant to be a professor. Harsh/rigid grading and closed-note tests aren't sensible for a coding class, and tests (that she made) not graded for 1-2 months. She tries to do too much then penalizes the students when she finds out she can't.

SHE IS A GOOD TEACHER. People need to chill on her because she is genuinely good at her job, but she teaches a hard class so people are bound to get bad grades. She does her best, and while it takes her a long time to grade, she is very generous with extra credit. You are going to struggle in this class if you dont like STATS, but she does her best

I do not understand other reviews. Is she the best professor? No, but she's a fine one. It's clear that she's adjusting to teaching here, but, her exams were quite fair. You'll struggle if you don't show up to class/study, but you'll do fine if you pay attention. Not an easy A, but you'll get a good grade if you show up and put in a little effort.

You need the textbook to survive this class bc she will just read off slides in the most boring manner. She is nice, but that is different from good. She is poorly organized and is constantly going "woe is me!" bc of how poorly she plans and manages her time. Haven't gotten multiple assignments graded (from months ago) and the final is in two days.

analysis.name: Ryan Urquhart
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 25 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: The other reviews aren't wrong, he definitely gets cross with the class sometimes. However, he did a good job of putting us in an entrepreneurial mindset and I got more out of this class than I was expecting. He also offered to write us a LoR for grad school which was unprecedented. Was he my favorite prof? No, but he far exceeded my expectations.

Amazing professor. If you're struggling in this class, it's not on him it's on you. He's tough, blunt, and expects you to act like an adult. People who complain are just soft and not ready for real expectations. If you can "handle" honesty and want to actually learn, take his class. Highly recommend.

I read the comments and had some apprehension coming into the class. I was amazed about how the comments were totally wrong. I'm a Jr and many of the ppl were Sr. who were complaining. All you had to do was come to class, complete the assignments. He was always available to meet via zoom, b4 or after class. Don't pay attn to the crybaby comments

This class was a huge waste of time. I heard that 382 with other professors is actually really helpful but I feel like I didn't learn anything. he was not clear at all on what he wanted and graded harshly. Didn't learn much he seems like a nice guy but takes himself too serious and doesn't teach any material. You have to cheat on quizzes due to ts

Super student‑focused. He invited feedback throughout the term and actually used it. His real‑world examples helped the material stick, and he made sure everyone participated. Would definitely take his class again. To stay in the loop, you must come to class.

analysis.name: Uffe Bergeton
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 46 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Dr Bergeton clearly cares a lot about the content but his lectures were not very captivating as someone who didn't already have a passion for the topic. I did enjoy the freedom in the final paper which allowed me to dive into really any aspect of Qin and Han history/culture. If you already know you love the topic, you'll enjoy this more than I did.

He definitely knows what he's talking about, but is terrible at teaching a simplified version of it. He goes off on really long unrelated tangents and is generally confusing. Definitely go to office hours because he is picky. Not to mention, he is white but claims to be "Chinese" as a result of marrying a Chinese woman.

There are quite a lot of readings, but they all relate in a way and most of the readings are online. 2 essays, one being the midterm, and the final being a 10pg paper along with a 5 min presentation. He is a very generous grader and the lectures were pretty interesting. He holds office hours for essay critique so you can score better on the essays.
hard course but he is a nice guy.the materials are hard though, i don't know the stories in the slides even though I'm a Chinese
uffe is so nice in class and is really gentle during lectures, but the grading is absolutely horrible. TA also grades really irresponsibly.

analysis.name: Beril Ulku-Steiner
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 45 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Had a phony and fake nice personality in lecture but if you approached her one on one, she was inflexible both in her availability and in her willingness to help outside of class. She assigns an case study paper with vague and unclear parameters and expectations, and she has questions on the test from videos we watched in class weeks earlier. SUCK!

Dr. U-S will come to class every day cheery and smiley but DONT LET IT FOOL YOU. It is mandatory to go to class even though the way you will learn the material is purely through reading - she does not help. She will assign a group project and about 11 pg paper but don't count on a good grade, she'll rip you on both no doubt. Tests are ok if you stu

She smiles a lot; don't let it fool you.  Instructions for things are completely unclear.  EXTREMELY harsh grader, tests on things that we never talked about in class.  Scope of information way too large.  Extremely picky.  She was late to every meeting I had with her (meetings that she required), didn't show up, or just postponed them last minute.

Although she was a bubbly professor at 8 AM, I learned very quickly that turns into a very condescending demeanor. She grades incredibly tough and does not give good feedback. It's really disappointing considering how much I was looking forward to this class. The lectures were mandatory but not helpful, pretty much taught myself all the material.

If you can avoid taking Professor US for Abnormal Psyc (or any class), then PLEASE DO. Horrible professor. Ridiculous grader. The group project and accompanying paper was a complete joke, as she graded unfairly, extremely harsh and her rubric was unclear. Don't let the happy demeanor fool you. Also, 1 unexcused abstence failing attendance grade.

analysis.name: Elaine Townsend Utin
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: AMAZING! Elaine is a true gem and I would HIGHLY recommend anyone to take her class. It is full of so much helpful and important information and she has passion behind everything she does. She is also super compassionate, and understanding of the chaos that was 2020. She held plenty of spaces for healing and for growth in combination with learning.

As an Education Professor Elaine really knows how to effectively foster a nurturing learning environment where everybody feels heard and respected. She is sweet and fun and lights up every class with her bright attitude and passion for what she teaches. She may honestly be too good for the classes she teaches. She takes radical pedagogy seriously.

Profe is amazing and a true gem at UNC. Her class is so interesting and a must take for any Latine person at UNC. It is a great class to explore your own identity and understand the world around you. Profe creates a safe space for people to be vulnerable and honest about their experiences. You really begin to feel at home in class.

analysis.name: James Umbanhowar
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Terrible.  Not even worth going to class.  I started reading the book before the class lectures and I started to think I could have taught the class better than he did.  He stutters a lot and spends the vast majority of the time talking about simple concepts and completely avoids the nitty-gritty details.  I also hated his voice.
Absolutely terrible.  Lectures made 0 sense. The recitations are the only thing that  saved me from failing

It is true that his lectures are a little vague and he speaks really fast but there is a lot information that he has to cover in a small amount of time. He tries his best to be helpful and make the class interesting and overall is not a bad professor. The class is tough but the exams are not impossible and the curve is usually pretty good.

Umbanhowar's lectures are worthless. He stands up there and discusses vague examples and graphs, and doesn't have a clear point. It's better to just read the textbook to understand the material. The tests aren't too hard and are curved ALOT because everyone does poorly. I would stay away from him and his poor ecology teachings.

You definitely need to have a good understanding of stats to take this class (and understand it). You can get by with all of the assignments by going to office hours and simply doing the assignments (those are 80% of the grade and no exams), but you won't really understand it without a baseline understanding of stats.
